CO-1.appealNotes: Patient owes per plan deductible. Verify amount against Summary of Benefits.

CO-2.appealNotes: Patient owes per plan coinsurance percentage.

CO-3.appealNotes: Patient owes per plan copay. Verify against plan SBC.

CO-4.description: Procedure code inconsistent with modifier or required modifier missing.
CO-4.appealNotes: Often a provider coding error. Ask provider to resubmit with corrected modifier. Common with modifier 25, 59, or 51.

CO-5.description: Professional claim submitted on wrong claim form or billed in wrong setting.
CO-5.appealNotes: Provider billing error. Contact provider to resubmit on correct form (CMS-1500 vs UB-04).

CO-6.appealNotes: If service was genuinely provided twice on the same day, ask provider to add modifier 76 (repeat procedure) on resubmission.

CO-11.appealNotes: Common denial worth appealing. Request peer-to-peer review. Your physician should write a letter connecting the diagnosis to the procedure.

CO-15.appealNotes: If service was an emergency, insurers must cover regardless of PA. Also check if provider failed to obtain PA — they may owe you a refund.

CO-16.description: Claim lacks information needed for adjudication.
CO-16.appealNotes: Usually a paperwork issue. Contact provider to resubmit with missing information (NPI, date of birth, etc.).

CO-18.appealNotes: If not a true duplicate, supply claim numbers for both and show they are different services.

CO-19.description: Claim is denied because this is a work-related injury/illness.
CO-19.appealNotes: If this is not work-related, provide documentation. Workers comp should only apply if employer confirmed an on-the-job injury.

CO-22.appealNotes: Coordination of Benefits (COB) issue. Contact insurer to confirm correct primary/secondary plan order.

CO-23.description: Claim submitted outside the filing deadline.
CO-23.appealNotes: Appealable if provider filed late. Provider may be liable — check your EOB date and provider's submission date.

CO-26.appealNotes: Service occurred before your effective date. Verify your coverage start date.

CO-27.appealNotes: Service occurred after coverage ended. Verify termination date and COBRA eligibility.

CO-29.description: Patient not eligible on service date.
CO-29.appealNotes: Verify your enrollment dates. If error on insurer side, request eligibility correction with HR or insurer.

CO-45.description: Charge exceeds fee schedule or maximum allowable.
CO-45.appealNotes: Contractual write-off between insurer and provider. You should NOT be billed for this amount. If provider balance-bills you, that may violate your plan.

CO-50.description: Non-covered service — payer determined service not medically necessary.
CO-50.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP or specialty guidelines. ER visits for infectious, cardiac, or neurologic diagnoses are almost always upheld. Request peer-to-peer review between your physician and the reviewing physician.

CO-51.description: Non-covered service — not deemed a medical necessity by the provider.
CO-51.appealNotes: Similar to CO-50. Requires physician letter of medical necessity.

CO-54.description: Multiple physicians/assistants are not covered in this case.
CO-54.appealNotes: If assistant surgeon or co-surgeon was required, physician should document medical necessity of their role.

CO-55.appealNotes: Request full clinical criteria from insurer. If procedure is FDA-approved and guideline-supported, cite published literature and peer guidelines.

CO-57.description: Payment adjusted because the patient has not met the required eligibility, spend down, or waiting period.
CO-57.appealNotes: Verify deductible and waiting period status. If you believe deductible is met, request itemized year-to-date ledger.

CO-58.description: Treatment was deemed not related to the primary diagnosis.
CO-58.appealNotes: Physician should write a letter documenting clinical relationship between diagnosis and treatment.

CO-59.description: Processed based on multiple surgery rules.
CO-59.appealNotes: If procedures were truly independent and not bundled, add modifier 59 (distinct procedural service) on resubmission.

CO-96.description: Non-covered charge(s). Charges are not covered by this plan.
CO-96.appealNotes: Benefit exclusion. Review your plan document to confirm this service is excluded, then explore FSA/HSA coverage.

CO-97.description: Payment included in another service already adjudicated.
CO-97.appealNotes: Check for unbundling violations. If service was distinct, modifier 59 may be appropriate on resubmission.

CO-109.description: Claim not covered by this payer — patient may be covered by another plan.
CO-109.appealNotes: Coordination of Benefits issue. Confirm primary/secondary plan order with both insurers.

CO-119.description: Benefit maximum for this time period has been reached.
CO-119.appealNotes: Plan maximum reached. Verify against your plan's benefit limits. ACA plans cannot have lifetime maximums for essential health benefits.

CO-167.appealNotes: Request the specific diagnosis codes payer considers non-covered. If diagnosis is misclassified, ask provider to correct and resubmit.

CO-197.appealNotes: Appealable if emergency or if PA was actually obtained. Provide PA number from provider records. Insurers must cover emergency services without PA under ACA.

CO-204.description: Service/equipment/drug is not covered under the patient's current benefit plan.
CO-204.appealNotes: Benefit exclusion. Review plan document. Ask prescribing physician about covered alternatives.

CO-252.description: An attachment or document is required to adjudicate this claim.
CO-252.appealNotes: Paperwork issue. Contact provider to submit the required documentation (operative report, records, etc.).

OA-18.appealNotes: Provide documentation showing services are distinct if not a true duplicate.

OA-23.description: Payment adjusted due to MSP (Medicare Secondary Payer) rules.
OA-23.appealNotes: MSP coordination issue. Contact Medicare and your insurer to confirm correct payer order.

PR-1.appealNotes: Patient owes. Verify amount against your plan Summary of Benefits and year-to-date deductible tracking.

PR-2.appealNotes: Patient owes per plan coinsurance percentage after deductible is met.

PR-3.appealNotes: Patient owes copay per plan terms.

PR-204.description: Service not covered by this payer — patient responsible.
PR-204.appealNotes: If you believe this is covered, request the specific exclusion language from your plan document.

CO-29.description: The time limit for filing has expired.
CO-29.appealNotes: Appealable if you can document the claim was submitted on time or that a delay was caused by circumstances beyond your control (e.g., coordination of benefits dispute, provider billing error). Request proof of timely filing from your provider.

CO-100.description: Payment adjusted because the benefit for this service/claim is not covered when performed by this type of provider, in this type of facility, or by this type of practitioner.
CO-100.appealNotes: For out-of-network emergency services, appeal citing state/federal surprise billing protections and the No Surprises Act. Emergency care must be covered at in-network cost-sharing rates regardless of network status.
